message:
perf(server): cache health counts with 30s TTL, add dispatch admission control

Health endpoint: enrich_readiness_response now caches project/message
COUNT(*) results for 30 seconds via a static LazyLock<Mutex<...>>,
matching the codebase's existing dashboard caching pattern. The mutex
is released before I/O to avoid holding the lock during slow queries.
Load-balancer health probes that poll every few seconds now hit cache
instead of running COUNT(*) each time.

Dispatch: adds an AtomicU32-based admission control (RAII DispatchPermit)
with a limit of 128 concurrent spawn_blocking dispatches. When the limit
is reached, new requests get an immediate MCP InternalError ("Server
overloaded") instead of queueing behind the blocking pool. The permit
is held inside the spawn_blocking closure, so the counter tracks actual
in-flight blocking threads, not just pending futures. This prevents
unbounded thread accumulation when timeouts fire but blocked threads
continue running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
